— pick your vibe

MarcFjor.

DC  ·  New York  ·  Elsewhere
— ethos

My job is to tell the best story possible and to move people without them knowing.

We are post-reality. We choose which narratives we believe each day. Often they are the ones that appear most sexy.

We aren't what we know, but what we consume — each of us reverting to our default settings at end of day, because no one's got time to ponder complexities when they are trying to make rent.

The full story is messy, with more ands and buts than anyone wants to sit with. There is no shortage of facts, and even those aren't objective.

We live under the most manipulative algorithms of all time. Each person lives in their own bubble, breathing more of the same breath.

I want to pop it, one jab at a time. Gift people air they didn't know they needed and hear the gasps.

I am building bridges so others can join me in the future I want to see, and I sincerely hope that this mirror I'm building reflects us both.

— philosophies
01
Branding

Branding's for companies, not people. And the two ought not be conflated.

02
Yellow Cake

We are much too obsessed with comprehension. Sometimes it's better to let a thought be understood. Stop the conversation at "I get what you mean" — no but, no and. Leave it at Yellow Cake.

03
2 Star · 5 Star

Go for it. Make people feel something. An audience should never leave feeling meh.

04
Mutation

Be uncertain. Do the work. Make one-offs, then put it out. Sometimes good things happen at random.

— photography
— open mic
▶
Open Mic
poetry · live
— poetry
Boundaries
... what if instead of shrinking, I grew? so much so that I squished everything and everyone into a wall with not a spare square foot to claim? what if I didn't laugh as a courtesy but instead rose to leave immediately, preferring my own company to that of tyrants? what if I chose me so much that I held a fuck, felt its weight, & didthatshit anyway? what would that look like? to not wallow, tiptoe or creep but dominate problems & let them be the ones to cower?
— yellow cake
YC
Yellow Cake
Writing, thinking, and things worth sitting with.
Published on Substack.

— i just called to say i love you
2023
I Just Called To Say I Love You
poet · contributing artist
A tribute to Black matriarchy and motherhood through the words and lens of four artists, curated by Cousin Tito. A 4ft × 20ft wheat paste mural at 1925 W North Avenue, West Baltimore.
Marc Fjor
[email protected]
© 2025

Chicken Lady

playwright · in development

Chicken Lady is a full-length dystopian satire about a Black family in DC whose lives get tangled up with a corporation that turned their mother into a brand. She signed a deal she couldn't get out of, and when she couldn't perform anymore, the company came after her son.

It's a play about artistic exploitation, gentrification, and what happens when the only people who can put you back together are the ones who actually know your name.

The whole piece is built from real DC voices and runs on a constant soundscape of construction. The city is literally being rebuilt around these characters as they fight to hold onto who they are.

in vein of
The Colored Museum
Bamboozled (2000)
Hollywood Shuffle (1987)
For inquiries: [email protected]
